data.title: European indexes decline on risk-off mood, macro jitters
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.40% to 10,557. The British pound traded at $1.341, slipping from last week’s one-month high of $1.348.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.94% to 23,581. 

France (CAC:IND) -1.00% to 8,176.

In other parts of Europe, Finland posted a current account deficit of €249M in February.

The Netherlands’ trade surplus widened to €9.9B in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.68% lower to 610.6, as risk aversion intensified after U.S.–Iran peace talks collapsed and Washington announced a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. 

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was down 2 basis points to 4.33%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 4.85%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 3.06%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Tesla wins first European approval for FSD supervised
data.description:
Dutch regulators have cleared the use of Tesla’s (TSLA) self-driving software with mandatory human supervision on highways and city streets, marking a European first for the EV maker as it pushes for broader adoption across the European Union.

The Netherlands’ approval of the system, branded Full Self-Driving Supervised, which can steer, brake, and accelerate, follows more than 18 months of testing and evaluation by the Dutch vehicle authority RDW.

"Proper use of this driver assistance system makes a positive contribution to road safety," RDW said in a statement on Friday, adding that ⁠it would also submit an application for the technology to be used throughout the EU.

RDW noted that EU vehicle approval standards impose stricter safety requirements than those in the U.S. "This means that the FSD Supervised version in the U.S. is NOT comparable to the FSD Supervised version in the EU," it said, without providing further details.

"We're excited to bring FSD Supervised to more European countries soon," Tesla said in a post on social media platform X, adding that it will begin rolling out the technology in the Netherlands shortly.

Widespread rollout of full self-driving is central to Tesla’s long-term growth strategy. A significant portion of its $1T valuation is tied to CEO Elon Musk’s bet that AI-driven autonomous software and robotaxis will evolve into a major revenue stream.

The company is also counting on FSD to help revive vehicle demand, which has slowed in Europe amid an aging EV lineup and backlash tied to Musk’s far-right political rhetoric.

Tesla’s (TSLA) European sales showed tentative signs of recovery, rising in February for the first time in over a year.

TSLA shares were up -0.48% premarket at $347.31. Shares have fallen nearly -22% YTD.

data.title: IBM to pay $17M to settle U.S. probe over DEI-related allegations
data.description:
• International Business Machines (IBM) has agreed to pay $17M to settle allegations that it violated the False Claims Act by failing to meet anti-discrimination requirements in federal contracts tied to alleged bias in hiring and employment practices.
  • "As a condition to being a federal contractor, the company must certify that it will not discriminate against an employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, national origin, or sex and must further certify that it will take steps to ensure that applicants are employed, and employees are treated during employment, without regard to race, color, national origin, or sex," according to a U.S. Department of Justice statement.
  • The settlement resolves allegations that IBM failed to comply with these requirements and knowingly maintained practices that the United States contends were discriminatory employment practices.

data.title: Stock index futures slide as U.S.-Iran talks fail; Trump looks to blockade Iran's ports
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Monday after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed and Washington announced it will begin a blockade of Iranian ports.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) dropped 0.55%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) declined 0.46%, and Dow futures (INDU) slipped 0.37%.

U.S. Central Command posted Sunday that it will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on all traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports starting Monday, April 13, at 10 a.m. ET, following a presidential directive.

Treasury yields were mixed across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) edged lower to 4.33%, down 0.8 basis points. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.82%, up 1.4 basis points, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked higher to 4.92%, up 0.7 basis points.

On the economic calendar, investors will watch the existing home sales report during market hours.

data.title: Lobe Sciences launches non-brokered private placement of 14.62M shares at $0.065
Oil could spike to $150 if US proceeds with Hormuz blockade: Onyx
data.description:
Oil prices are significantly underpriced and should be much higher than current levels if the U.S. proceeds with a planned naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, according to a veteran oil market executive.

Brent crude (CO1:COM) climbed above $103 a barrel on Monday, before settling at $101.6, after weekend talks between Washington and Tehran failed to produce a deal, intensifying an already fragile global energy backdrop. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (CL1:COM) climbed over 7% to roughly $104.

"The number we saw this morning — $103; 8% increase — is not reflective at all of what could happen if the US really decides to go with this interdiction,” Onyx Capital Group managing director Jorge Montepeque said on Bloomberg Television, “It really makes no sense. It should be $140, $150.”

U.S. forces are set to begin enforcing the blockade at 10 a.m. New York time Monday, targeting all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports. Montepeque warned the move could turn a regional conflict into a global one, potentially disrupting up to 12M bbl/day of supply and triggering a sharp repricing in oil markets.

“This is, in one word: demented,” Montepeque said in the interview . “It makes no sense what the US is doing because they are so focused on Iran that they are losing sight of what they are causing to the world. And the pain is in Asia, the pain is in the South Pacific, the pain is in anybody that depends on oil.”

Oil prices may be around $100 a barrel for rest of the year if Trump dials back some of his actions, Montepeque added.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: KKR ramps up Japan real estate bets as asset sales accelerate
data.description:
KKR's (KKR) Japan real estate management subsidiary, KJRM Holdings, plans expansion in purchases of properties that Japanese companies want to sell off—a market the firm estimates at ¥450T, or $2.8T. 

"Corporate demand to sell real estate is very strong due partly to shareholder activists and that will likely be the case for three to five years," according to KJRM Holdings’ president, Naoki Suzuki said in an interview, the Japan Times reported. "The trend of companies selling off their real estate holdings to improve capital efficiency will continue."

The U.S.-based investment firm is positioning its Japan-focused property platform to capture a growing wave of disposals as local corporations face mounting pressure. Suzuki added that more than half of the properties acquired in recent years by KJRM-managed real estate investment trusts and private funds were sourced from corporate divestments.

The Japanese unit is focusing on inflation‑resistant, high‑cash‑flow properties in major cities such as Tokyo, Osaka, and Nagoya, betting that higher rents can offset rising interest rates and borrowing costs.

data.title: Aon raises quarterly dividend by 10.1% to $0.82 a share
data.description:
• Aon (AON) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, 10.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.745.
  • Forward yield 1.05%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record May 1; ex-div May 1.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 10.1% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.745 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See AON Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lomiko Metals appoints Belinda Labatte as interim CFO
UK homebuilder Vistry appoints Adam Daniels CEO after Greg Fitzgerald steps down
data.description:
• Vistry Group (BVHMY) has appointed Adam Daniels as CEO with immediate effect, after executive chairman Greg Fitzgerald handed in his notice last month, the company said on Monday.
  • The FTSE 250 housebuilder said Daniels, currently executive chair of one of two largest regional divisions, will also join the board as an executive director.
  • Fitzgerald, ⁠who had been ​due to retire as ​chairman at May's annual meeting and remain CEO for up ​to 12 months ​while a successor was found, will ‌step ⁠down from both roles immediately by mutual agreement, the company said.
  • The faster-than-expected transition comes after a difficult period for Vistry (BVHMF), which recently cautioned that margins will stay under pressure into 2026 amid weak demand in the UK housing market.
